name: dataclass-factory

metadata.name: dataclass-factory
classifiers: License :: OSI Approved :: Apache Software License
metadata.summary: An utility class for creating instances of dataclasses

documentation:
# dataclass_factory

[![PyPI version](https://badge.fury.io/py/dataclass-factory.svg)](https://badge.fury.io/py/dataclass-factory)
[![Build Status](https://travis-ci.org/Tishka17/dataclass_factory.svg?branch=master)](https://travis-ci.org/Tishka17/dataclass_factory)
[![downloads](https://img.shields.io/pypi/dm/dataclass_factory.svg)](https://pypistats.org/packages/dataclass_factory)
[![license](https://img.shields.io/github/license/Tishka17/dataclass_factory.svg)](https://github.com/Tishka17/dataclass_factory/blob/master/LICENSE)

**dataclass_factory** is a modern way to convert dataclasses or other objects to and from more common types like dicts

## Help

See [documentation](https://dataclass-factory.readthedocs.io/) for more details.

## TL;DR

Install
```bash
pip install dataclass_factory
```

Use
```python
from dataclasses import dataclass
import dataclass_factory


@dataclass
class Book:
    title: str
    price: int
    author: str = "Unknown author"


data = {
    "title": "Fahrenheit 451",
    "price": 100,
}

factory = dataclass_factory.Factory()
book: Book = factory.load(data, Book)  # Same as Book(title="Fahrenheit 451", price=100)
serialized = factory.dump(book) 
``` 

## Requirements

* python >= 3.6

You can use `dataclass_factory` with python 3.6 and `dataclass` library installed from pip. 

On python 3.7 it has no external dependencies outside of the Python standard library.

## Advantages

* No schemas or configuration needed for simple cases. Just create `Factory` and call `load`/`dump` methods
* Speed. It is up to 10 times faster than `marshmallow` and `dataclasses.asdict` (see [benchmarks](benchmarks))
* Automatic name style conversion (e.g. `snake_case` to `CamelCase`)
* Automatic skipping of "internal use" fields (with leading underscore)
* Enums, typed dicts, tuples and lists are supported out of the box
* Unions and Optionals are supported without need to define them in schema
* Generic dataclasses can be automatically parsed as well
* Cyclic-referenced structures (such as linked-lists or trees) also can be converted
* Validators, custom parser steps are supported.
* Multiple schemas for single type can be provided to support different ways of parsing of the same type

functions.name: dataclass_factory.path_utils.replace_ellipsis
documentation: Fix all `...` in the path replacing then with the name.

functions.name: dataclass_factory.path_utils.init_structure
documentation:
Create empty structure that can be filled by described path
Returns whole container itself and separate subcontainers for each path

variables.name: dataclass_factory.path_utils.ellipsis
documentation: noqa F821

variables.name: dataclass_factory.path_utils.CleanPath
documentation: normal value

modules.name: dataclass_factory.validators
documentation: field_parser -> validator(field_parser(pre_validator))

functions.name: dataclass_factory.validators.validate
documentation:
Decorator to set a method as a data validator.
Such method will be called during data parsing.

Validator method receives data which must be validated
and returns corrected data or raises ValueError

```
param fields

names of fields (as they are in target class)
which are processed by this validator.
None is treated as "any single field"

param pre

flag to call validator before parsing corresponding value
```

functions.name: dataclass_factory.fields.schema_fields_filter
documentation:
Check if field is allowed by a schema.

* field is in `only` list (if provided some)
* field is not excluded

functions.name: dataclass_factory.fields.get_dataclass_fields
documentation: wrappers

class_variables.name: dataclass_factory.fields.BaseFieldInfo.type
documentation: noqa A003

classes.name: dataclass_factory.common.AbstractFactory
documentation: Facade class to retrieve data converters

classes.name: dataclass_factory.factory.Factory

methods.name: dataclass_factory.factory.Factory.__init__
documentation:
---
param debug_path: |-
  show path to broken field in parsing exceptions
  (InvalidFieldError will be raised)
param default_schema: schema used if no specific schema is provided
param json_schema_definitions_path: |-
  path to definitions of jsonschemas
  in overall schema, used by \$ref
param schemas: |-
  dictionary with specific schemas for classes
  that can be converted. Settings from default schema
  will be used if they are not set in more specific one
---

methods.name: dataclass_factory.factory.Factory.schema
documentation: Finds or creates `Schema` describing `class_` conversion rules

methods.name: dataclass_factory.factory.Factory.parser
documentation:
Returns preconfigure parser to create `class_` instances
from simple data structures using previously set schemas

methods.name: dataclass_factory.factory.Factory.json_schema_ref_name
documentation:
Create name of jsonschema reference used to locate description
of `class_` in overall schema

methods.name: dataclass_factory.factory.Factory.json_schema
documentation:
Create json schema describing `class_`.
Can contain references to other classes

methods.name: dataclass_factory.factory.Factory.json_schema_definitions
documentation:
Create mapping for all crated json schemas

Note: it is filled only with schemas which are requested at least
once and their references

methods.name: dataclass_factory.factory.Factory.serializer
documentation:
Returns preconfigured serializer to convert `class_` instances
to simple data structures using previously set schemas

methods.name: dataclass_factory.factory.Factory.load
documentation: Create `class_` instance form `data`

methods.name: dataclass_factory.factory.Factory.dump
documentation:
Convert `data` to plain structures.
If `class_` is not provided then `type(data)` will be used
Facade class for all data conversion operations

classes.name: dataclass_factory.naming.NameStyle
documentation:
Enumeration to describe which styles do field names fit
in plain (serialized/unparsed) structure

functions.name: dataclass_factory.generics.fix_generic_alias
documentation:
This function normalizes generics created via aliases.
E.g. `List[List[T]][int]` should replaced via `List[List[int]]`

On each call it normalizes one level, so it is recurrent

variables.name: dataclass_factory.parsers.MISSED
documentation: field is missed in parsed data

functions.name: dataclass_factory.serializers.get_complex_serializer
documentation:
---
param getter: functions used to get data for each field (attribute, key and so on)
param omit_missing: omit special MISSING values retrieved from getter. Is applied
  when all defaults are MISSING.
---

functions.name: dataclass_factory.type_detection.get_self_type_hints
documentation: Returns type hints declared in current class without inherited once

variables.name: dataclass_factory.type_detection.UnionType
documentation: type: ignore

classes.name: dataclass_factory.schema.Schema
documentation:
Class describing data conversion rules.
See documentation for more details.

In case of inheriting you can set any setting as a class field.
Callable settings can be just methods.
